national_indicator_available: Tax reliefs and direct transfers of funds
comparison_sdg: The time series are compliant with the UN metadata.
indicator_name: Amount of fossil-fuel subsidies (production and consumption) per unit of GDP
target_name: Rationalize inefficient fossil-fuel subsidies that encourage wasteful consumption by removing market distortions, in accordance with national circumstances, including by restructuring taxation and phasing out those harmful subsidies, where they exist, to reflect their environmental impacts, taking fully into account the specific needs and conditions of developing countries and minimizing the possible adverse impacts on their development in a manner that protects the poor and the affected communities
un_designated_tier_url_alert: der Statistischen Division der Vereinten Nationen
goal_meta_link_alert: the Statistical Devision of the UN
source_organisation_1: Federal Statistical Office (Destatis)
source_url_text_1: Data on the Taxed Energy Products Statistics (only available in German)
source_url_text_1b: Data on the Environmental Tax Revenues Statistics of the Environmental Economic Accounting

footer_fields.label: Notes
footer_fields.value:
• Tax reliefs (indirect subsidies): Government revenue foregone due to tax relief, such as reduced energy tax rates, tax exemptions for certain industries, or special depreciation allowances.
• Direct financial assistance (direct subsidies): Direct cash payments from the government, such as grants to businesses, investment aid, support programs in the areas of energy or industrial policy, and compensation payments.
• Data is only available from 2015.

footer_fields.label: Notes
footer_fields.value:
• Tax reliefs (indirect subsidies): Government revenue foregone due to tax relief, such as reduced energy tax rates, tax exemptions for certain industries, or special depreciation allowances.
• Direct financial assistance (direct subsidies): Direct cash payments from the government, such as grants to businesses, investment aid, support programs in the areas of energy or industrial policy, and compensation payments.
• Data is only available from 2015.
• GDP: Gross domestic product.

graph_title: Tax reliefs and direct transfers of funds
data_last_update: Last updated on 2024/06/05. Data for further reporting periods will be added as soon as it becomes available.
